title: Mitigating urban heat island through neighboring rural land cover

data_scope.city_type: monocentric Chinese cities >200 km2 (avg equivalent diameter ~22 km)
data_scope.lst_source: MODIS MOD11A1 V6.1 LST_Day_1km (~1 km, summer)
data_scope.spatial_design: 5 urban ladders (UDI) x 4 rural ladders (2.5-20 km from boundary)

core_conclusion: Urban heat extends beyond the city edge, so neighboring rural land cover can cool the whole city. Across 30 Chinese cities (2000-2020), rural land within ~10-15 km of the urban boundary (about half the city's equivalent diameter) had the strongest effect, explaining up to ~30% of surface UHI intensity variance in the urban core. The number and adjacency of landscape patches are the two key levers, each able to reduce UHI by up to 0.5 C; recommended designs avoid fragmentation, raise shape complexity, and make patch distribution more uniform.
why_it_matters: Reframes UHI mitigation beyond scarce urban land toward the surrounding countryside as a cooling reservoir; pins down where (the ~10-15 km rural ring) and which landscape knobs (patch number, adjacency, cohesion, shape) deliver cooling; yields planner-actionable, transferable design rules linking urban and rural development.
why_nature: Transferable mechanism tested across 30 diverse cities and climate zones; grounds the empirical result in heat-island circulation physics; strongly policy-translatable landscape-design recommendations; couples space, climate and human well-being.
added_value_to_my_research: Confounder-disentangling SHAP/ALE template -> sharper attribution in explainable spatial models and MGWR
Patch-graph physics-informed operator -> reusable GeoAI solver for accessibility/flow graphs
Population-weighted cooling surface -> heat-exposure covariate for health-access models and equity framing
Rural-layout optimization -> multi-objective efficiency-equity facility-location with peri-urban land lever
Time-series LST + reanalysis fusion -> spatiotemporal heat early-warning via STKDE and multi-source fusion
